behavior_summary: Implements a stationary 'spitter' entity that reacts to being looked at with a short bark/description and, when a valid actor enters its fast-area trigger, repeatedly spawns and fires small projectile 'spit' entities toward that actor until the actor leaves the area.
U8P_SPITTER::enterFastArea marks per-item info/state and spawns a repeating spitting process (U8P_SPITTER::00DE) tied to the activator/owner.
U8P_SPITTER::leaveFastArea clears the info/state and calls FREE::2A56 to free/stop the spawned spitting process, cleaning up behavior when the actor leaves.

U8P_SPITTER::00DE is a looping process that waits a randomized delay (urandom), optionally spawns a short-timer/free helper (FREE::1C19) to implement the wait, then triggers U8P_SPITTER::011C to create and hurl a spit projectile; this repeats while the process is active.

U8P_SPITTER::011C computes direction to the target (Item::getDirToItem), maps the direction into X/Y velocity components (with randomization via urandom), creates the projectile item (Item::create), tags it (Item::orStatus), plays a SFX (playSFX), places it in the world (Item::pop) and applies velocity (Item::hurl).
notable_control_flow: Look handler (U8P_SPITTER::look) is an event-driven short handler: set per-item info/process-exclude, call Item::bark("spitter ") and suspend/return.
Fast-area entry (U8P_SPITTER::enterFastArea) spawns a persistent repeating process (U8P_SPITTER::00DE) associated with the spitter/activator so spitting only occurs while an actor is in range.
Fast-area leave (U8P_SPITTER::leaveFastArea) invokes FREE::2A56 with the stored process/info reference to stop and clean up the repeating spitting process.

The spitting loop (U8P_SPITTER::00DE) uses urandom to vary timing, spawns a short-timer helper (FREE::1C19) to implement delays, then spawns the concrete firing routine U8P_SPITTER::011C in a repeating cycle.
open_questions: Exact semantics of the various per-item info keys (constants 0x0205, 0x0208, 0x021B, 0x021C) — they appear to mark process ownership/exclusion but precise engine semantics are inferred.
Precise behavior and side-effects of FREE::1C19 (spawned from the delay computation) — it appears to implement a timed wait or auxiliary visual/logic effect but its exact role is uncertain.

Range interpretation and unit meaning of velocity and timing constants (e.g., urandom ranges 0xC8, 0x14, 0x1E, 0x0F; spawn offsets like +0x18 / -0x0B) — numeric effects on gameplay (speed/distance/time) are inferred from usage.

Whether the spawned spitting process (U8P_SPITTER::00DE) targets only the first entering actor or updates target if another actor enters while already active (target management is inferred from stored/indirect arguments but not explicit).

main_function.name: U8P_SPITTER::look
main_function.purpose: Provide immediate feedback when an actor looks at the spitter by emitting a short descriptive bark and setting per-item info/process-exclude markers during the handler.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_SPITTER::look) — invoked when the Avatar or another actor examines/looks at the spitter item.

function_map.name: U8P_SPITTER::look
function_map.role: Short event handler for 'U8P_SPITTER::look' that barks a description and sets per-item info/process-exclude.

function_map.name: U8P_SPITTER::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area entry trigger: marks item info/process-exclude and spawns the repeating spitting process (U8P_SPITTER::00DE).

function_map.name: U8P_SPITTER::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area exit trigger: clears marks and calls FREE::2A56 to stop/free the spawned spitting process.

function_map.name: U8P_SPITTER::00DE
function_map.role: Persistent loop/process that waits randomized intervals and repeatedly triggers projectile creation (U8P_SPITTER::011C) while active.

function_map.name: U8P_SPITTER::011C
function_map.role: Instantiates, positions, and hurls a spit projectile toward the target; includes direction lookup and randomized velocity mapping.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine-provided function used by U8P_SPITTER::look handler to display/emote the spitter's short description string to the activator/viewer.

internal_calls.purpose: Spawn the repeating spitting process bound to the activator/spitter so it starts firing projectiles while an actor is in the fast-area.
Trigger a single firing action (create + hurl projectile) as part of the spitting loop.

external_calls.purpose: Emit the literal description 'spitter ' to the activator/viewer as response to U8P_SPITTER::look.
Called on leave to free/stop the spawned spitting process or clear stored process info (cleanup).
Compute randomized wait/delay values between spits to vary firing cadence.
Spawned with a computed small delay/value; likely used to implement a timed wait or timeout during the spitting loop.
Determine discrete direction (0..7) from spitter to target to orient projectile velocity.
Randomize projectile speed components and minor timing/offset variations for less deterministic projectiles.
Create the projectile item instance (type 0x0324) and return its pointer into a local out-parameter.
Set status bits (mask 0x02) on the created projectile to mark ownership/state before spawning.
Play projectile firing sound (sfx id 0x64) when spitter emits a spit.
Read spitter Z to compute projectile spawn height (Z + 0x18 offset observed).
Read spitter Y to compute projectile spawn Y (Y - 0x0B offset observed).
Read spitter X to compute projectile spawn X (X - 0x0B offset observed).
Place the created projectile item into the world at computed X,Y,Z coordinates.
Apply computed X/Y velocity components (with randomized magnitude) to the projectile so it moves toward the target.

writes.name: per-item info keys (e.g., 0x0205, 0x0208, 0x021B, 0x021C)
writes.likely_effect: Set on the spitter/activator to record process ownership, exclusion from normal processing, and to associate spawned processes; used to start/stop the repeating spitting behavior.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Implements a stationary 'spitter' entity that reacts to being looked at with a short bark/description and, when a valid actor enters its fast-area trigger, repeatedly spawns and fires small projectile 'spit' entities toward that actor until the actor leaves the area.
